description: Per-baseline OTA eligibility for the HP TouchPad (Topaz) update server. Keyed on the device fingerprint (device-scripts/fingerprint.sh), NEVER on build number (all baselines report build 86; LunaCE/pollution make it unreliable).

gates.comment: Universal preconditions evaluated from the fingerprint VERDICT. If the verdict is any of these, NO package is delivered — the whole plan is empty.

refuse_verdicts.UNSUPPORTED: Wrong product (BUILDNAME != Nova-HP-Topaz — e.g. TouchPad Go/Nova-Dubonnet, AT&T 4G/Nova-ATT-Topaz). Different hardware and base image. NEVER push.
refuse_verdicts.HAZARD: Supported Topaz but unsafe state: a custom community kernel (org.webosinternals.kernels.*, e.g. UberKernel) is installed. Our OTA rewrites /boot/uImage and post-update reverts to the stock kernel, which would clobber it. Do not push.
refuse_verdicts.UNKNOWN: Topaz, but not a defined baseline (undefined L/T/Q combo) or an unrecognized LunaSysMgr build. Hold for manual review; do not push.

capabilities_from_fingerprint.comment: Which capability a device already HAS, derived from the fingerprint axes L/T/Q.

evidence_watch.comment: Logged by the fingerprint; does not gate on its own, but flagged per-package for conflict review.
evidence_watch.optware_openssl: mobi.optware.openssl — vestigial /opt-only 0.9.8. Owns no /usr files, not in the linker path, no ipkg Conflicts. Harmless to our OTA; no action.
evidence_watch.ausmt_patches: org.webosinternals.patches.* — warn-and-log. A patch that edits a file one of our packages also ships is a conflict risk; see each package's conflict_watch.

packages.title: Community TLS 1.3 stack
packages.notes: Core modernization: private /usr/lib/ssl11 stack + current root certs + clock sync. Low-risk (leaves system OpenSSL untouched). Delivered to devices that LACK modern TLS (T==0): baselines C and D.

packages.title: Legacy system OpenSSL swap (0.9.8k -> 1.0.2u)
packages.notes: Replaces the SYSTEM libssl/libcrypto that 15+ components link against -> boot/brick risk (openssl-update-plan.md). Superseded by the private ssl11 stack, which the community adopted instead. Kept as packages/openssl-update_1.0.2u-1_armv7.off for reference only; NEVER delivered to any baseline.

packages.title: QupZilla modern browser (nizovn Qt5)
packages.notes: OPT-IN enhancement, not auto-pushed. Heavy (Qt5 + glibc stack). Candidate offer for devices lacking a modern browser (Q==0: baselines B, D, E). Product decision pending.

packages.title: LunaCE launcher
conflict_watch: luna-tls13 launcher edit (documented brick when combined)
packages.notes: OPT-IN only, never auto-pushed. Swaps /usr/bin/LunaSysMgr; documented brick when stacked with the ssl11 launcher edit. Candidate offer for L==0 devices (B, D). Requires standalone per-device testing; product decision pending.
